# UX Checklist: Use assessment baseline

**Purpose**: Validate that UI flows present baseline-anchored estimates, provenance, and warnings clearly.
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: `specs/016-assessment-baseline/spec.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/prompts:speckit.checklist` workflow.

## Result Presentation

- [x] CHK-UX-001 Baseline value and provenance fields are displayed in the result view.
- [x] CHK-UX-002 Final estimate is framed as “Assessment baseline + adjustments”.
- [x] CHK-UX-003 Adjustment breakdown is shown when available, or explicitly labeled when omitted.
- [x] CHK-UX-004 Traceability identifier (correlation/request ID) is visible in the result view.

## Warnings & States

- [x] CHK-UX-005 Ambiguous match selection is disclosed with a user-facing warning.
- [x] CHK-UX-006 Missing/stale baseline states show fallback labeling or explicit error messaging.
- [x] CHK-UX-007 Partial-feature availability shows missing categories without implying zero impact.
- [x] CHK-UX-008 Guardrail application shows low-confidence or equivalent indicator.

## Consistency & Accessibility

- [x] CHK-UX-009 Labels match shared naming conventions: “Assessment baseline”, “Final estimate”, “Adjustments”.
- [x] CHK-UX-010 Basic accessibility pass (keyboard navigation + contrast) is part of the UX gate.
